info.title: Distributed Job Scheduler
info.description: A Postgres-backed distributed job scheduling platform. Postgres is both the source of truth and the queue — jobs are claimed atomically with `SELECT ... FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED`. All routes below `/api/v1` require a JWT bearer token except `/auth/*`. Errors use a uniform envelope: `{"error": {"code", "message", "details"}}`.

post.summary: Register a new user (bootstraps a personal org + owner membership)
Exchange credentials for a JWT access token
Submit a job (immediate or delayed; idempotent replay returns 200)
Submit an array of jobs atomically (single transaction)
Bulk re-enqueue a queue's failed/dead jobs (fresh, attempts reset)

get.summary: Project throughput, success rate, p50/p95 latency, and queue depth

tags.name: auth
tags.description: Registration, login, and the current-user probe.

tags.name: projects
tags.description: Org-scoped projects that group queues and jobs.

tags.name: queues
tags.description: Queue config, pause/resume, retry policies, and stats.

tags.name: jobs
tags.description: Submit, list, inspect, cancel, batch, and bulk-retry jobs.

tags.name: metrics
tags.description: Throughput, success rate, latency percentiles, depth.
